title: pleopeltis aurea
intro: Pleopeltis aurea, also known as the goldback polypody, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pleopeltis aurea include the potential treatment of various ailments such as arthritis, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in traditional medicine.
